content.problemStatement:
For an array AAA of size NNN, we define f(A)f(A)f(A) as follows:


If AAA contains NNN distinct elements, f(A)=0f(A) = 0f(A)=0.
Otherwise, f(A)=min‚Å°(j‚àíi)f(A) = \min (j - i)f(A)=min(j‚àíi) among all pairs (i,j)(i, j)(i,j) satisfying 1‚â§i<j‚â§N1 \le i < j \le N1‚â§i<j‚â§N and Ai=AjA_i = A_jAi‚Äã=Aj‚Äã.
You are given 222 integers NNN and KKK.

Your task is to find the sum of f(A)f(A)f(A) over all arrays AAA of size NNN satisfying 1‚â§Ai‚â§K1 \le A_i \le K1‚â§Ai‚Äã‚â§K. Since the answer may be large, print it modulo 998244353998244353998244353.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
The first and only line of input contains 222 integers - NNN and KKK.


Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the sum of f(A)f(A)f(A) over all arrays AAA of size NNN and 1‚â§Ai‚â§K1 \le A_i \le K1‚â§Ai‚Äã‚â§K modulo 998244353998244353998244353.
constraints: The sum of NNN and the sum of KKK over all test cases does not exceed 2‚ãÖ1052 \cdot 10^52‚ãÖ105

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test Case 1 : There is only one array A=[1,1]A = [1, 1]A=[1,1], and this has f(A)=1f(A) = 1f(A)=1 corresponding to the pair (i,j)=(1,2)(i, j) = (1, 2)(i,j)=(1,2).
Test Case 2 : There are 444 arrays. 222 of them, i.e. [1,2][1, 2][1,2] and [2,1][2, 1][2,1], have f(A)=0f(A) = 0f(A)=0 due to all elements distinct. The other 222 arrays [1,1][1, 1][1,1] and [2,2][2, 2][2,2] have f(A)=1f(A) = 1f(A)=1.
